claim: The 5y5y forward rate is contaminated by 50-75bp of fiscal term premium, overstating the market's neutral rate estimate
needs: Model-independent decomposition of fiscal vs. non-fiscal term premium components. Counterfactual for fiscal-specific component vs. historical norms. Cross-validation across ACM and Kim-Wright frameworks.

claim: The Fama-French HML factor carries ~1-2% annualized excess return attributable to fiscal policy
needs: Identification strategy separating fiscal policy effects from coincident early-cycle recovery and inflation expectations. t-stats (2.1-2.4) must survive multiple-hypothesis correction with N~6 episodes. Larger episode sample or natural experiment.

claim: ACM vs. Kim-Wright term premium model divergence (30-50bp) is itself a diagnostic signal of fiscal regime uncertainty
needs: Formal testing of whether the inter-model divergence specifically reflects fiscal supply vs. expectations driver-mix shifts, rather than general model specification differences. Out-of-sample validation.

claim: The bilateral U.S.-China fiscal accelerator adds an estimated 0.5-1.0% GDP to combined fiscal commitments per decade
needs: Methodology for the magnitude estimate. Historical calibration of bilateral fiscal competition dynamics. Identification of de-escalation scenarios and their probability.

claim: Central bank independence erosion operates through appointment dynamics on a 4-8 year cycle, with the U.S. currently at early stage
needs: Comparative institutional analysis of U.S. vs. EM precedents (Turkey, India, Hungary). Assessment of Senate confirmation friction and Fed cultural resistance. Market-based indicators of independence erosion.

claim: AI capex ($200-250B/yr from hyperscalers) may partially substitute for the Kalecki fiscal channel in supporting corporate profits
needs: Determination of whether AI capex is self-sustaining or bubble-dependent. NIPA-level decomposition of AI capex contribution to corporate profits. Framework for assessing substitutability between fiscal and private investment channels.

claim: The tariff-fiscal nexus is net-negative for deficit reduction, with revenue gains offset by GDP drag
needs: Net fiscal impact modeling under specific effective tariff rate assumptions. Trade elasticity estimation at current tariff levels. Retaliation scenario analysis. Resolution of 2018-19 ~55% realization rate applicability to current regime.

claim: Private credit opacity ($1.8T) represents the critical unknown Minsky variable
needs: Unquantifiable from public data by definition. Would require: regulatory reporting requirements for private credit funds, independent mark-to-market audits, PIK toggle prevalence data. Stress event may be the only mechanism for information revelation.

claim: The crowded duration short (CFTC 95th percentile) creates contrarian mean-reversion risk on a 3-6 month tactical horizon
needs: Separation of tactical positioning signal from structural term premium trajectory. Confirmation that 7/10 historical hit rate (positive 6-month returns at 90th+ percentile shorts since 1990) holds in current regime. Assessment of whether a 50bp tactical unwind is consistent with a structural grind higher.
